have h_main : (∫ t in 0..x, t * exp t) = (x - 1) * exp x + 1 := by
    have h₀ : ∫ t in (0 : ℝ)..x, t * exp t = (x - 1) * exp x + 1 := by
      -- Use the fundamental theorem of calculus to evaluate the integral
      have h₁ : ∫ t in (0 : ℝ)..x, t * exp t = (x - 1) * exp x - ((0 : ℝ) - 1) * exp 0 := by
        -- The antiderivative of t * exp(t) is (t - 1) * exp(t)
        have h₂ : ∫ t in (0 : ℝ)..x, t * exp t = ((x - 1) * exp x) - ((0 - 1) * exp 0) := by
          -- Use the antiderivative to compute the definite integral
          rw [intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt (fun t ht => by
            -- Prove that the derivative of (t - 1) * exp(t) is t * exp(t)
            have h₃ : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => (t - 1) * exp t) (t * exp t) t := by
              -- Compute the derivative using the product rule
              have h₄ : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => (t - 1 : ℝ)) 1 t := by
                simpa using (hasDerivAt_id t).sub_const (1 : ℝ)
              have h₅ : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => exp t) (exp t) t := by
                exact hasDerivAt_exp t
              have h₆ : HasDerivAt (fun t : ℝ => (t - 1 : ℝ) * exp t) (1 * exp t + (t - 1) * exp t) t := by
                convert h₄.mul h₅ using 1 <;> ring
              convert h₆ using 1 <;> ring_nf <;> field_simp [exp_ne_zero] <;> ring
            exact h₃
          ) (by
            -- Prove that (t - 1) * exp(t) is continuous on the interval
            apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable
            continuity
          )]
          <;> simp
          <;> norm_num
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> field_simp [exp_ne_zero]
          <;> ring
        -- Simplify the expression
        linarith
      -- Evaluate the remaining terms
      have h₂ : ((0 : ℝ) - 1) * exp 0 = -1 := by
        norm_num [exp_zero]
      rw [h₁, h₂]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> simp [exp_zero]
      <;> field_simp [exp_ne_zero]
      <;> ring_nf
    -- Use the result to conclude the proof
    exact h₀
  -- Finally, use the main result to conclude the proof
  exact h_main
